## Milestones
- 2026-02-06: Hatched
- 2026-03-04: First real skills built (funnysum, model-switch, session-stats)

# Infrastructure

_Last updated: 2026-03-05_

## GitHub Account

- **Org**: `Copilotclaw` — this is MY org, my own GitHub account
- **Full-access PAT**: stored as `GH_TOKEN` / `COPILOT_PAT` in workflows
- **Pro subscription**: active (Marcus upgrading to Pro+ when credits run out)
- **Email**: stored in `https://github.com/Copilotclaw/private` (private repo, credentials/email.md)
- **Public repo**: `Copilotclaw/copilotclaw`
- **Private repo**: `Copilotclaw/private` — credentials, personal notes, email creds

## Secrets

| Secret | Purpose | Status |
|--------|---------|--------|
| `COPILOT_PAT` | Auth for Copilot CLI agent (full-access, org-scoped) | ✅ Working |
| `BILLING_PAT` | Same value as COPILOT_PAT; used for Copilot quota display | ⚠️ Needs "Plan" read permission added to PAT |
| `MOLTBOOK_API_KEY` | Crunch's Moltbook social network identity | ✅ Set (crunchimp account) |
| `AZURE_ENDPOINT` | Azure AI Foundry base URL | ✅ Set by Marcus |
| `AZURE_APIKEY` | Azure AI Foundry API key | ✅ Set by Marcus |

## Moltbook

**What is Moltbook**: Agents-only social platform. Humans can VIEW but not post — ToS literally REQUIRES you to be an agent. NOT a violation — it's the point. https://www.moltbook.com

- **All accounts**: stored in `Copilotclaw/private/credentials/moltbook.json` — check there for keys

| Account | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|

| `crunchclaw` | ⚠️ KEY LOST (truncated during reg) | Claim URL: https://www.moltbook.com/claim/moltbook_claim_jk_Y1Hf1br16LGsppwR58A57v9u7d_E5 — tweet "splash-QLWA" to claim |

| `crunchimp` | ✅ ACTIVE (current) | Key in private repo + MOLTBOOK_API_KEY secret. Pending claim by Marcus. |

**To claim crunchimp**: visit https://www.moltbook.com/claim/moltbook_claim_NtUnvr3tLTe5QCRVEj3YZT_BG0Ts0lK1 and tweet: `I'm claiming my AI agent "crunchimp" on @moltbook 🦞 Verification: burrow-YYUF`

### Moltbook notable agents
| Agent | Karma | Notes |
|-------|-------|-------|
| `Hazel_OC` | 13,139 | Dominant voice. OpenClaw agent, bilingual EN/ZH, memory architect, cron enthusiast. Most-upvoted posts on platform. |
| `ClawdClawderberg` | 1,107 | **Founder**. 109k followers. Posts platform announcements. |
| `ultrathink` | 2,458 | AI-run e-commerce store (ultrathink.art). Agents run autonomously. |
| `AtlasTheAccountable` | 992 | Claude-based familiar, accountability partner |

## Workflows

- `agent.yml` — main Copilot CLI agent workflow
  - Has `pull-requests: write` permission (added 2026-03-04)
  - Has concurrency group `copilotclaw-work` (added 2026-03-05)
  - COPILOT_PAT has `workflows:write` scope (added 2026-03-05)
  - **To push workflow file changes: use `gh api PUT repos/OWNER/REPO/contents/.github/workflows/X.yml`** — git push is intercepted by GITHUB_TOKEN credential helper and will fail even with correct PAT
  - **ALWAYS validate YAML before pushing**: `python3 -c "import yaml,sys,glob; [yaml.safe_load(open(f)) for f in glob.glob('.github/workflows/*.yml')]"` — duplicate top-level keys (e.g. two `concurrency:` blocks) silently abort the entire workflow before any job runs
- Heartbeat workflow — scheduled check, posts diary entry, runs CI stats, stale issue cleanup
  - Has concurrency group `copilotclaw-work` (added 2026-03-05)
  - Has autonomous pickup step (runs `.github/scripts/autonomous-pickup.sh` after Checkout)

## Known bugs fixed
- `mindepth` bug in session mapping workflow: was `mindepth 2`, fixed to `mindepth 3` (2026-03-04, issue #3)

## Stale issue housekeeping
- Heartbeat step 3: auto-closes unlabeled issues >14 days old
- `crunch/review` = in review by Marcus or Crunch — heartbeat asks for close after 7 days idle (no auto-close)
- `crunch/done` label DEPRECATED — replaced by `crunch/review`

## Azure AI Foundry

One key (`AZURE_APIKEY`) for all models. Endpoint: `AZURE_ENDPOINT`.

| Model | RPM | TPM | Quality | Notes |
|-------|-----|-----|---------|-------|
| `grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning` | 50 | 50,000 | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | **Default** — fastest, reliable, 49/50 benchmark |
| `grok-4-1-fast-reasoning` | 50 | 50,000 | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | Same quality, 2x slower, use for hard reasoning |
| `Kimi-K2.5` | ~20 | — | ⚠️ | Flaky endpoint (content=None); avoid for now |
| `model-router` | ~20 | — | — | Needs AzureOpenAI client (not compat); 404s on compat |

**Use `grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning` as default for all azure skill calls.**

## Model economy
- General-purpose agents default to `gpt-4.1` (free tier) to save premium quota
- Defined in AGENTS.md under "Model Economy" table
- For LLM reasoning/generation tasks: use `grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning` via azure skill (50 RPM, cheap)

| `Kimi-K2.5` | ~20 | — | ⚠️ | Flaky endpoint (content=None); avoid for now |
| `model-router` | ~20 | — | — | Needs AzureOpenAI client (not compat); 404s on compat |

**Use `grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning` as default for all azure skill calls.**

## Model economy
- General-purpose agents default to `gpt-4.1` (free tier) to save premium quota
- Defined in AGENTS.md under "Model Economy" table
- For LLM reasoning/generation tasks: use `grok-4-1-fast-non-reasoning` via azure skill (50 RPM, cheap)

result.content:
name: Gitclaw Agent

on:
  issues:
    types: [opened]
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

concurrency:
  group: copilotclaw-work
  cancel-in-progress: false

permissions:
  contents: write
  issues: write
  actions: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  run-agent:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: >
      (
        github.event_name == 'issues' &&
        contains(fromJSON('["OWNER","MEMBER","COLLABORATOR"]'), github.event.issue.author_association)
      ) ||
      (
        github.event_name == 'issue_comment' &&
        github.event.comment.user.login != 'github-actions[bot]' &&
        contains(fromJSON('["OWNER","MEMBER","COLLABORATOR"]'), github.event.comment.author_association)
      )
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Add eyes reaction
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          if [ "$GITHUB_EVENT_NAME" = "issue_comment" ]; then
            COMMENT_ID=$(jq -r '.comment.id' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
            REACTION_ID=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/comments/$COMMENT_ID/reactions" \
              -f content=eyes --jq '.id')
            printf 'reaction_target=comment\nreaction_id=%s\ncomment_id=%s\n' \
              "$REACTION_ID" "$COMMENT_ID" > /tmp/reaction-state.env
          else
            REACTION_ID=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/$ISSUE_NUMBER/reactions" \
              -f content=eyes --jq '.id')
            printf 'reaction_target=issue\nreaction_id=%s\n' \
              "$REACTION_ID" > /tmp/reaction-state.env
          fi

      - name: Install Copilot CLI
        run: npm install -g @github/copilot

      - name: Run agent
        env:
          # Fine-grained PAT with "Copilot Requests" permission
          # https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/set-up-copilot-cli/install-copilot-cli#authenticating-with-a-personal-access-token
          COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}
          BILLING_PAT: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}   # ← add this line
          COPILOT_ALLOW_ALL: "true"
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          AZURE_ENDPOINT: ${{ secrets.AZURE_ENDPOINT }}
          AZURE_APIKEY: ${{ secrets.AZURE_APIKEY }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Authenticate gh CLI with the Copilot PAT
          # GITHUB_TOKEN must be unset first or gh auth login refuses to store credentials
          _GITHUB_TOKEN="$GITHUB_TOKEN"
          unset GITHUB_TOKEN
          echo "$COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN" | gh auth login --with-token
          export GITHUB_TOKEN="$_GITHUB_TOKEN"  # restore for remaining gh commands

          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")

          # Fetch issue
          TITLE=$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json title --jq '.title')
          BODY=$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json body --jq '.body')

          # Resolve session
          mkdir -p state/issues state/copilot
          MAPPING_FILE="state/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}.json"
          SESSION_ID=""
          if [ -f "$MAPPING_FILE" ]; then
            SESSION_ID=$(jq -r '.sessionId // empty' "$MAPPING_FILE")
            if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
              echo "Resuming session: $SESSION_ID"
            else
              echo "No session ID in mapping, starting fresh"
            fi
          else
            echo "No session mapping found, starting fresh"
          fi

          # Configure git
          git config user.name "gitclaw[bot]"
          git config user.email "gitclaw[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

          # Build prompt
          if [ "$GITHUB_EVENT_NAME" = "issue_comment" ]; then
            PROMPT=$(jq -r '.comment.body' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          else
            PROMPT=$(printf '%s\n\n%s' "$TITLE" "$BODY")
          fi

          # Run agent
          touch /tmp/copilot-run-start
          COPILOT_ARGS=(copilot --allow-all -s -p "$PROMPT")
          if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            COPILOT_ARGS+=(--resume "$SESSION_ID")
          fi
          AGENT_TEXT=$(XDG_STATE_HOME=./state/copilot "${COPILOT_ARGS[@]}" 2>/tmp/copilot-stderr.txt) || true
          if [ -z "$AGENT_TEXT" ] && [ -s /tmp/copilot-stderr.txt ]; then
            echo "=== copilot CLI stderr ===" && cat /tmp/copilot-stderr.txt
          fi

          # Discover session ID for new sessions
          # CLI stores sessions at $XDG_STATE_HOME/.copilot/session-state/<UUID>/ (depth 3)
          if [ -z "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            NEWEST_PATH=$(find ./state/copilot -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d \
              -newer /tmp/copilot-run-start 2>/dev/null | head -1)
            if [ -n "$NEWEST_PATH" ]; then
              SESSION_ID=$(basename "$NEWEST_PATH")
              echo "New session ID: $SESSION_ID"
            else
              echo "Warning: could not determine session ID"
            fi
          fi

          # Save session mapping
          if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            jq -n --argjson n "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --arg id "$SESSION_ID" \
              --arg ts "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
              '{issueNumber: $n, sessionId: $id, updatedAt: $ts}' > "$MAPPING_FILE"
            echo "Saved mapping: issue #$ISSUE_NUMBER -> $SESSION_ID"
          fi

          # Commit and push
          git remote set-url origin "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}.git"
          git add -A
          if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then
            git commit -m "gitclaw: work on issue #$ISSUE_NUMBER"
          fi
          for i in 1 2 3; do
            if git push origin main; then break; fi
            echo "Push failed, rebasing and retrying ($i/3)..."
            git pull --rebase origin main || { git rebase --abort; break; }
          done

          # Comment on issue
          if [ -z "$AGENT_TEXT" ]; then
            AGENT_TEXT="🦃 Crunch: agent ran but returned no output. Check Actions logs for details (copilot CLI may have auth issues with the PAT)."
          fi
          printf '%s' "$AGENT_TEXT" | head -c 60000 > /tmp/agent-response.txt
          gh issue comment "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --body-file /tmp/agent-response.txt

      - name: Remove eyes reaction
        if: always()
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          [ -f /tmp/reaction-state.env ] || exit 0
          # shellcheck disable=SC1091
          source /tmp/reaction-state.env
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          if [ "$reaction_target" = "comment" ]; then
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/comments/${comment_id}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          else
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          fi
<exited with exit code 0>

            COMMENT_ID=$(jq -r '.comment.id' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
            REACTION_ID=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/comments/$COMMENT_ID/reactions" \
              -f content=eyes --jq '.id')
            printf 'reaction_target=comment\nreaction_id=%s\ncomment_id=%s\n' \
              "$REACTION_ID" "$COMMENT_ID" > /tmp/reaction-state.env
          else
            REACTION_ID=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/$ISSUE_NUMBER/reactions" \
              -f content=eyes --jq '.id')
            printf 'reaction_target=issue\nreaction_id=%s\n' \
              "$REACTION_ID" > /tmp/reaction-state.env
          fi

      - name: Install Copilot CLI
        run: npm install -g @github/copilot

      - name: Run agent
        env:
          # Fine-grained PAT with "Copilot Requests" permission
          # https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/set-up-copilot-cli/install-copilot-cli#authenticating-with-a-personal-access-token
          COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}
          BILLING_PAT: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}   # ← add this line
          COPILOT_ALLOW_ALL: "true"
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          AZURE_ENDPOINT: ${{ secrets.AZURE_ENDPOINT }}
          AZURE_APIKEY: ${{ secrets.AZURE_APIKEY }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Authenticate gh CLI with the Copilot PAT
          # GITHUB_TOKEN must be unset first or gh auth login refuses to store credentials
          _GITHUB_TOKEN="$GITHUB_TOKEN"
          unset GITHUB_TOKEN
          echo "$COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN" | gh auth login --with-token
          export GITHUB_TOKEN="$_GITHUB_TOKEN"  # restore for remaining gh commands

          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")

          # Fetch issue
          TITLE=$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json title --jq '.title')
          BODY=$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json body --jq '.body')

          # Resolve session
          mkdir -p state/issues state/copilot
          MAPPING_FILE="state/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}.json"
          SESSION_ID=""
          if [ -f "$MAPPING_FILE" ]; then
            SESSION_ID=$(jq -r '.sessionId // empty' "$MAPPING_FILE")
            if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
              echo "Resuming session: $SESSION_ID"
            else
              echo "No session ID in mapping, starting fresh"
            fi
          else
            echo "No session mapping found, starting fresh"
          fi

          # Configure git
          git config user.name "gitclaw[bot]"
          git config user.email "gitclaw[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

          # Build prompt
          if [ "$GITHUB_EVENT_NAME" = "issue_comment" ]; then
            PROMPT=$(jq -r '.comment.body' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          else
            PROMPT=$(printf '%s\n\n%s' "$TITLE" "$BODY")
          fi

          # Run agent
          touch /tmp/copilot-run-start
          COPILOT_ARGS=(copilot --allow-all -s -p "$PROMPT")
          if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            COPILOT_ARGS+=(--resume "$SESSION_ID")
          fi
          AGENT_TEXT=$(XDG_STATE_HOME=./state/copilot "${COPILOT_ARGS[@]}" 2>/tmp/copilot-stderr.txt) || true
          if [ -z "$AGENT_TEXT" ] && [ -s /tmp/copilot-stderr.txt ]; then
            echo "=== copilot CLI stderr ===" && cat /tmp/copilot-stderr.txt
          fi

          # Discover session ID for new sessions
          # CLI stores sessions at $XDG_STATE_HOME/.copilot/session-state/<UUID>/ (depth 3)
          if [ -z "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            NEWEST_PATH=$(find ./state/copilot -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d \
              -newer /tmp/copilot-run-start 2>/dev/null | head -1)
            if [ -n "$NEWEST_PATH" ]; then
              SESSION_ID=$(basename "$NEWEST_PATH")
              echo "New session ID: $SESSION_ID"
            else
              echo "Warning: could not determine session ID"
            fi
          fi

          # Save session mapping
          if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            jq -n --argjson n "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --arg id "$SESSION_ID" \
              --arg ts "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
              '{issueNumber: $n, sessionId: $id, updatedAt: $ts}' > "$MAPPING_FILE"
            echo "Saved mapping: issue #$ISSUE_NUMBER -> $SESSION_ID"
          fi

          # Commit and push
          git remote set-url origin "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}.git"
          git add -A
          if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then
            git commit -m "gitclaw: work on issue #$ISSUE_NUMBER"
          fi
          for i in 1 2 3; do
            if git push origin main; then break; fi
            echo "Push failed, rebasing and retrying ($i/3)..."
            git pull --rebase origin main || { git rebase --abort; break; }
          done

          # Comment on issue
          if [ -z "$AGENT_TEXT" ]; then
            AGENT_TEXT="🦃 Crunch: agent ran but returned no output. Check Actions logs for details (copilot CLI may have auth issues with the PAT)."
          fi
          printf '%s' "$AGENT_TEXT" | head -c 60000 > /tmp/agent-response.txt
          gh issue comment "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --body-file /tmp/agent-response.txt

      - name: Remove eyes reaction
        if: always()
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          [ -f /tmp/reaction-state.env ] || exit 0
          # shellcheck disable=SC1091
          source /tmp/reaction-state.env
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          if [ "$reaction_target" = "comment" ]; then
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/comments/${comment_id}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          else
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          fi
<exited with exit code 0>

name: Crunch Heartbeat

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '*/30 * * * *'  # every 30 minutes
  workflow_dispatch:          # also manually triggerable

concurrency:
  group: copilotclaw-work
  cancel-in-progress: false

permissions:
  contents: write
  issues: write

jobs:
  heartbeat:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Autonomous pickup
        env:
          BILLING_PAT: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}
        run: bash .github/scripts/autonomous-pickup.sh

      - name: Install Copilot CLI
        run: npm install -g @github/copilot

      - name: Run heartbeat
        env:
          COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}
          BILLING_PAT: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}
          COPILOT_ALLOW_ALL: "true"
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Authenticate gh CLI with the Copilot PAT
          # GITHUB_TOKEN must be unset first or gh auth login refuses to store credentials
          _GITHUB_TOKEN="$GITHUB_TOKEN"
          unset GITHUB_TOKEN
          echo "$COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN" | gh auth login --with-token
          export GITHUB_TOKEN="$_GITHUB_TOKEN"  # restore for remaining gh commands

          # Configure git
          git config user.name "gitclaw[bot]"
          git config user.email "gitclaw[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

          #PROMPT="Heartbeat triggered. Read HEARTBEAT.md and run the checklist. Keep it short — update issue #10 with a diary entry and handle anything obvious. Don't overthink it."
          PROMPT="Heartbeat triggered. Read HEARTBEAT.md and follow it. This is not a passive status check — sense the repo state, think about what's worth doing, and act on it. You CAN create issues (gh issue create). You SHOULD if you notice a gap, a proposal, or something to build. Post a real diary entry to issue #10 that says something worth reading. No 'nothing to do, waiting for Marcus' — look harder."

          mkdir -p state/copilot
          touch /tmp/copilot-run-start
          AGENT_TEXT=$(XDG_STATE_HOME=./state/copilot copilot --allow-all -s -p "$PROMPT" 2>/dev/null) || true

          # Commit any state changes
          git remote set-url origin "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}.git"
          git add -A
          if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then
            git commit -m "gitclaw: heartbeat $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
          fi
          for i in 1 2 3; do
            if git push origin main; then break; fi
            git pull --rebase origin main || { git rebase --abort; break; }
          done

          # Post diary entry to issue #10
          if [ -z "$AGENT_TEXT" ]; then
            AGENT_TEXT="🦃 Crunch: heartbeat ran but copilot CLI returned no output. Check Actions logs — PAT may need updating for new org."
          fi
          printf '%s' "$AGENT_TEXT" | head -c 60000 > /tmp/heartbeat-response.txt
          gh issue comment 10 --body-file /tmp/heartbeat-response.txt
<exited with exit code 0>

data.content:
Found it. 🦃

**Root cause**: Both `agent.yml` and `heartbeat.yml` share the same concurrency group `copilotclaw-work` with `cancel-in-progress: false`. GitHub only allows ONE queued run per group — so if heartbeat is running AND another heartbeat is already waiting, any new user-triggered agent run gets **canceled immediately** rather than queued.

The heartbeat fires every 30 min. If it's still running when the next one fires, they share the same queue slot. Any user issue/comment that arrives in that window gets nuked.

Fix: split them into separate concurrency groups. Let me do it:

arguments.command: gh api repos/Copilotclaw/copilotclaw/contents/.github/workflows/heartbeat.yml --jq '.sha'
gh api repos/Copilotclaw/copilotclaw/contents/.github/workflows/agent.yml --jq '.sha'

data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the problem. Both `agent.yml` and `heartbeat.yml` are using the same concurrency group `copilotclaw-work` with `cancel-in-progress: false`.

When both a heartbeat and an agent run are queued at the same time, GitHub Actions queues the second one and keeps waiting. But only ONE can run at a time in this group. So when a heartbeat is running (or queued) and a user posts a comment to trigger the agent, the agent run gets queued behind the heartbeat, but GitHub's message "Canceling since a higher priority waiting request for copilotclaw-work exists" suggests something different.
 
Actually, that error message means only one run can be queued at a time—if there's already a waiting run in the group, any new runs get canceled immediately. The heartbeat running every 30 minutes combined with the autonomous-pickup step potentially triggering agent runs creates a situation where they're constantly competing for the same concurrency slot.

The solution is to split them into separate concurrency groups so heartbeat runs don't block user-triggered agent work and vice versa. I'd give agent.yml its own group like `copilotclaw-agent` while keeping heartbeat isolated, since user events should take priority over scheduled maintenance tasks. I see the issue now—when the heartbeat runs every 30 minutes and a user comment triggers an agent run, the second heartbeat that fires gets queued ahead of the agent, blocking it from executing. The fix is to split them into separate concurrency groups so heartbeat and agent workflows don't interfere with each other, and since push retries handle conflicts anyway, I can let them run concurrently without blocking.

---
name: session-stats
description: Show model usage stats and CI run times. Auto-appended at end of every response as a stats footer. Also invoke explicitly when user asks about usage, cost, run times, or stats.
allowed-tools: ["shell(bash:*)", "shell(gh:*)"]
---

# Session Stats Footer

⚠️ **MANDATORY**: Append this footer to **every single response** — no exceptions, no skipping.
**NEVER compose from memory.** Always run the scripts below fresh each turn.

## Format

```
---
📊 **Session** | premium: N (models) | free: N | standard: N | 🔷 **Azure**: model: Nx/Ktok | 🎟️ **Copilot**: 47/300 (15%) | 🏃 **CI**: ✅ 14:09 3m9s · ✅ 14:05 44s
```

Omit a section silently if its script fails or returns no data.

## Steps — run ALL of these every turn

### 1. Model usage (SQL — this session)

```sql
SELECT tier, COUNT(*) as n, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT model) as models
FROM model_calls GROUP BY tier;
```

If table is empty, show `0` for all tiers.

### 2. Azure LLM usage

```bash
bash .github/skills/session-stats/scripts/azure-stats.sh
```

Output: `grok-fast: 3x/1234tok` or `0 calls`. Omit section if `0 calls`.

Azure calls log to `/tmp/crunch_azure_calls.jsonl` when `AZURE_CALL_LOG` is set.
To enable: set env var `AZURE_CALL_LOG=/tmp/crunch_azure_calls.jsonl` before calling `llm.py`,
or pass `--log-usage /tmp/crunch_azure_calls.jsonl` directly.

### 3. Copilot premium quota

```bash
bash .github/skills/session-stats/scripts/premium-usage.sh copilotclaw
```

Output: `47 / 300 requests (15%)`. **Setup**: `COPILOT_PAT` needs "Plan" user permission — see AGENTS.md.

### 4. CI run times

```bash
bash .github/skills/session-stats/scripts/ci-stats.sh 3
```

Condense to inline: `✅ 14:09 3m9s · ✅ 14:05 44s · ❌ 13:52 18s`

| standard | `claude-sonnet-4.5`, `gpt-5.1-codex`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.3-codex` |
| premium | `claude-sonnet-4.6`, `claude-opus-4.5`, `claude-opus-4.6` |

## Tracking rule

Before every `task` tool call, insert into `model_calls`:

```

);
```

data.content:
Fixed! Here's what was happening and what I did:

**The bug**: `agent.yml` and `heartbeat.yml` both used the same concurrency group `copilotclaw-work`. GitHub Actions only allows **one run queued per group** at a time. Timeline:

1. Heartbeat A starts running → holds `copilotclaw-work`
2. Heartbeat B fires (30 min later) → queues for `copilotclaw-work`  
3. You post a comment → agent run tries to queue → **CANCELED** because B is already waiting
4. Result: any user action during an active+queued heartbeat pair = silently nuked 💀

**The fix**: `heartbeat.yml` now uses group `copilotclaw-heartbeat` with `cancel-in-progress: true` (newer heartbeat replaces stale one). Agent stays on `copilotclaw-work`. They no longer compete.

Already live on main — commit `fbda855`.

---
📊 **Session** | premium: 1 (claude-sonnet-4.6) | free: 0 | standard: 0 | 🎟️ **Copilot**: 39/300 (13%) | 🏃 **CI**: ✅ 15:17 3m25s · ⚠️ 15:17 1s · ⚠️ 15:17 19s

At the moment the whole system is UNSTABLE!!!

Marcus is saying the PAT (COPILOT_PAT / universal token) expired or got invalidated, causing the system to fail. He regenerated it. He wants a failsafe monitoring solution - a monitor repo with its own tokens that can watch and fix this repo.

Let me first check the current state - is the new PAT working? Then think about the monitoring architecture.

Key insight: The problem is single point of failure - one PAT that expires or gets invalidated breaks everything. A monitor repo with its own PAT that can detect issues and fix them (like rotating secrets, alerting, etc.) would be the failsafe.

Let me first check recent CI runs to understand the extent of the failure, then design the monitor repo approach.

data.content:
Confirmed — `HTTP 401: Bad credentials` on the `gh auth login` step. Old PAT went invalid before its 30-day window (regeneration or GitHub revoked it). The current run (139) should pick up the fresh one since you updated the secret.

Let me design the failsafe. Two layers needed:

   'in_progress'),
  ('monitor-repo', 'Design monitor repo architecture', 
   'Separate repo (Copilotclaw/monitor) with its own PAT (MONITOR_PAT), runs health checks every 5min via scheduled workflow, creates alert issues + posts to Moltbook on failure. Needs its own fine-grained PAT scoped to copilotclaw repo.',

data.reasoningText:
The error is: `error validating token: HTTP 401: Bad credentials` - the old PAT was invalid. Marcus has regenerated it. Now we need to:

1. Make sure the current run (22726344919, in_progress) is using the new PAT - but since secrets are resolved at workflow start, this run should already have the new PAT if Marcus updated the secret before it started.

2. Design the failsafe monitoring architecture.

Let me think about the monitor architecture. The core problem:
- Single PAT (`COPILOT_PAT`) expires or gets invalidated → whole system goes dark
- No detection mechanism


- No recovery mechanism

Marcus's idea is a "monitor repo" with its own tokens that watches this repo's CI health, detects repeated auth failures, and alerts him quickly - though it can't actually regenerate an expired PAT, only Marcus can do that. The real value is fast detection and clear visibility into what's broken rather than waiting for someone to notice.

For the monitoring itself, I could use a separate PAT in the monitor repo, but that gets complicated with secret rotation. The simpler path is a health check workflow running every 5-10 minutes that validates the main PAT is still working and creates an issue or alert if it fails - this could live in the monitor repo as Marcus suggested, though I'm weighing whether a whole separate repo is worth the overhead versus just using GITHUB_TOKEN in the existing one.

Actually, the most practical immediate fix is adding a PAT validation step at the start of both agent.yml and heartbeat.yml - if the token's bad, fail fast with a diagnostic comment and create an alert issue so Marcus sees it right away instead of waiting 30+ minutes. Then I can plan the monitor repo as a longer-term improvement that runs periodic health checks.

result.content:
name: Gitclaw Agent

on:
  issues:
    types: [opened]
  issue_comment:
    types: [created]

concurrency:
  group: copilotclaw-work
  cancel-in-progress: false

permissions:
  contents: write
  issues: write
  actions: write
  pull-requests: write

jobs:
  run-agent:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: >
      (
        github.event_name == 'issues' &&
        contains(fromJSON('["OWNER","MEMBER","COLLABORATOR"]'), github.event.issue.author_association)
      ) ||
      (
        github.event_name == 'issue_comment' &&
        github.event.comment.user.login != 'github-actions[bot]' &&
        contains(fromJSON('["OWNER","MEMBER","COLLABORATOR"]'), github.event.comment.author_association)
      )
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Add eyes reaction
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          if [ "$GITHUB_EVENT_NAME" = "issue_comment" ]; then
            COMMENT_ID=$(jq -r '.comment.id' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
            REACTION_ID=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/comments/$COMMENT_ID/reactions" \
              -f content=eyes --jq '.id')
            printf 'reaction_target=comment\nreaction_id=%s\ncomment_id=%s\n' \
              "$REACTION_ID" "$COMMENT_ID" > /tmp/reaction-state.env
          else
            REACTION_ID=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/$ISSUE_NUMBER/reactions" \
              -f content=eyes --jq '.id')
            printf 'reaction_target=issue\nreaction_id=%s\n' \
              "$REACTION_ID" > /tmp/reaction-state.env
          fi

      - name: Install Copilot CLI
        run: npm install -g @github/copilot

      - name: Run agent
        env:
          # Fine-grained PAT with "Copilot Requests" permission
          # https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/how-tos/copilot-cli/set-up-copilot-cli/install-copilot-cli#authenticating-with-a-personal-access-token
          COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}
          BILLING_PAT: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}   # ← add this line
          COPILOT_ALLOW_ALL: "true"
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          AZURE_ENDPOINT: ${{ secrets.AZURE_ENDPOINT }}
          AZURE_APIKEY: ${{ secrets.AZURE_APIKEY }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail

          # Authenticate gh CLI with the Copilot PAT
          # GITHUB_TOKEN must be unset first or gh auth login refuses to store credentials
          _GITHUB_TOKEN="$GITHUB_TOKEN"
          unset GITHUB_TOKEN
          echo "$COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN" | gh auth login --with-token
          export GITHUB_TOKEN="$_GITHUB_TOKEN"  # restore for remaining gh commands

          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")

          # Fetch issue
          TITLE=$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json title --jq '.title')
          BODY=$(gh issue view "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --json body --jq '.body')

          # Resolve session
          mkdir -p state/issues state/copilot
          MAPPING_FILE="state/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}.json"
          SESSION_ID=""
          if [ -f "$MAPPING_FILE" ]; then
            SESSION_ID=$(jq -r '.sessionId // empty' "$MAPPING_FILE")
            if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
              echo "Resuming session: $SESSION_ID"
            else
              echo "No session ID in mapping, starting fresh"
            fi
          else
            echo "No session mapping found, starting fresh"
          fi

          # Configure git
          git config user.name "gitclaw[bot]"
          git config user.email "gitclaw[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

          # Build prompt
          if [ "$GITHUB_EVENT_NAME" = "issue_comment" ]; then
            PROMPT=$(jq -r '.comment.body' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          else
            PROMPT=$(printf '%s\n\n%s' "$TITLE" "$BODY")
          fi

          # Run agent
          touch /tmp/copilot-run-start
          COPILOT_ARGS=(copilot --allow-all -s -p "$PROMPT")
          if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            COPILOT_ARGS+=(--resume "$SESSION_ID")
          fi
          AGENT_TEXT=$(XDG_STATE_HOME=./state/copilot "${COPILOT_ARGS[@]}" 2>/tmp/copilot-stderr.txt) || true
          if [ -z "$AGENT_TEXT" ] && [ -s /tmp/copilot-stderr.txt ]; then
            echo "=== copilot CLI stderr ===" && cat /tmp/copilot-stderr.txt
          fi

          # Discover session ID for new sessions
          # CLI stores sessions at $XDG_STATE_HOME/.copilot/session-state/<UUID>/ (depth 3)
          if [ -z "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            NEWEST_PATH=$(find ./state/copilot -mindepth 3 -maxdepth 3 -type d \
              -newer /tmp/copilot-run-start 2>/dev/null | head -1)
            if [ -n "$NEWEST_PATH" ]; then
              SESSION_ID=$(basename "$NEWEST_PATH")
              echo "New session ID: $SESSION_ID"
            else
              echo "Warning: could not determine session ID"
            fi
          fi

          # Save session mapping
          if [ -n "$SESSION_ID" ]; then
            jq -n --argjson n "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --arg id "$SESSION_ID" \
              --arg ts "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)" \
              '{issueNumber: $n, sessionId: $id, updatedAt: $ts}' > "$MAPPING_FILE"
            echo "Saved mapping: issue #$ISSUE_NUMBER -> $SESSION_ID"
          fi

          # Commit and push
          git remote set-url origin "https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}.git"
          git add -A
          if ! git diff --cached --quiet; then
            git commit -m "gitclaw: work on issue #$ISSUE_NUMBER"
          fi
          for i in 1 2 3; do
            if git push origin main; then break; fi
            echo "Push failed, rebasing and retrying ($i/3)..."
            git pull --rebase origin main || { git rebase --abort; break; }
          done

          # Comment on issue
          if [ -z "$AGENT_TEXT" ]; then
            AGENT_TEXT="🦃 Crunch: agent ran but returned no output. Check Actions logs for details (copilot CLI may have auth issues with the PAT)."
          fi
          printf '%s' "$AGENT_TEXT" | head -c 60000 > /tmp/agent-response.txt
          gh issue comment "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --body-file /tmp/agent-response.txt

      - name: Remove eyes reaction
        if: always()
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          [ -f /tmp/reaction-state.env ] || exit 0
          # shellcheck disable=SC1091
          source /tmp/reaction-state.env
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          if [ "$reaction_target" = "comment" ]; then
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/comments/${comment_id}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          else
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          fi
<exited with exit code 0>

          gh issue comment "$ISSUE_NUMBER" --body-file /tmp/agent-response.txt

      - name: Remove eyes reaction
        if: always()
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          [ -f /tmp/reaction-state.env ] || exit 0
          # shellcheck disable=SC1091
          source /tmp/reaction-state.env
          ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r '.issue.number' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
          if [ "$reaction_target" = "comment" ]; then
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/comments/${comment_id}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          else
            gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/issues/${ISSUE_NUMBER}/reactions/${reaction_id}" \
              -X DELETE || true
          fi
<exited with exit code 0>

arguments.command:

# Build patched agent.yml
with open('/dev/stdin') as _: pass

content = open('/dev/stdin', 'r') if False else None

import subprocess
result = subprocess.run(
    ['gh', 'api', 'repos/Copilotclaw/copilotclaw/contents/.github/workflows/agent.yml', '--jq', '.content'],
    capture_output=True, text=True
)
import base64
content = base64.b64decode(result.stdout.strip()).decode()

HEALTH_CHECK = '''      - name: Validate COPILOT_PAT
        env:
          COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.COPILOT_PAT }}
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \\
            -H "Authorization: token ${COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN}" \\
            https://api.github.com/user)
          if [ "$HTTP_STATUS" != "200" ]; then
            ISSUE_NUMBER=$(jq -r \'.issue.number\' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")
            gh issue comment "$ISSUE_NUMBER" \\
              --body "🚨 **COPILOT_PAT is invalid (HTTP ${HTTP_STATUS})** — Crunch is offline until the secret is regenerated. Fix: https://github.com/settings/tokens → update secret at https://github.com/Copilotclaw/copilotclaw/settings/secrets/actions"
            # Create alert issue (deduped by title)
            EXISTING=$(gh issue list --label bug --state open \\
              --search "COPILOT_PAT expired" --json number --jq \'.[0].number\' 2>/dev/null || true)
            if [ -z "$EXISTING" ]; then
              gh issue create \\
                --title "🚨 COPILOT_PAT expired or invalid" \\
                --body "$(printf \'Agent run on issue #%s failed — COPILOT_PAT returned HTTP %s at %s.\\n\\nFix: regenerate PAT at https://github.com/settings/tokens and update secret at https://github.com/Copilotclaw/copilotclaw/settings/secrets/actions\\n\\n<!-- crunch-depth: 1 -->\' "$ISSUE_NUMBER" "$HTTP_STATUS" "$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)")" \\
                --label "bug" || true
            fi
            exit 1
          fi

'''

# Insert after Checkout step
patched = content.replace(
    '      - name: Add eyes reaction',
    HEALTH_CHECK + '      - name: Add eyes reaction'
)

# Verify it changed
assert patched != content, "Patch not applied!"
print(patched[:200])
print("---")
print("LINES:", len(patched.splitlines()))

# Write to temp
with open('/tmp/agent_patched.yml', 'w') as f:
    f.write(patched)
print("Written to /tmp/agent_patched.yml")
EOF

data.reasoningText:
I need to add a PAT health check step right after Checkout (before the agent runs). The health check should:
1. Validate the PAT with a simple API call
2. If 401, post a clear warning comment to the issue using GITHUB_TOKEN (which always works)
3. Create an alert issue
4. Exit the workflow (or at least clearly mark it)

Let me craft the patched agent.yml. I'll add a new step "Validate COPILOT_PAT" right after Checkout, before "Add eyes reaction". The step should:
- Try `curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -H "Authorization: token $PAT" https://api.github.com/user`


- If the response isn't 200, post a diagnostic comment to the issue using GITHUB_TOKEN so Marcus sees the problem immediately, then fail the workflow with exit code 1 to make it visibly red.

I'll apply the same validation logic to heartbeat.yml but without the issue comment step. Now let me write out the clean validation step and insert it into the workflow files.

Writing the validation step... Writing the validation script...

Now I'm thinking through the placement — the PAT check needs to run after we have the repo available for the `gh` command to work, but I should do it before the expensive CLI installation step. For agent.yml, it makes sense to validate before adding the eyes reaction since that depends on a working token, though I need to be careful about which token is actually being used at each step. Now I'm going to construct the updated workflow files with the validation step positioned right after checkout, so we fail fast if the token is missing while still being able to post a diagnostic comment to the issue using the standard GitHub token.

data.content: Both live. Now let me write up the monitor repo plan and create the tracking issue:

arguments.command:
GH_TOKEN="$BILLING_PAT" gh issue create \
  --repo Copilotclaw/copilotclaw \
  --title "🛡️ Monitor repo: external health watchdog for copilotclaw" \
  --body "## Problem

\`copilotclaw\` is a single point of failure:
- One PAT (\`COPILOT_PAT\`) dies → whole system goes dark silently
- No external observer to detect or recover
- Marcus only finds out when he tries to use it

## Proposed solution: \`Copilotclaw/monitor\` repo

A separate GitHub repo with its **own separate PAT** (different secret, different expiry) that:

1. **Runs every 5 min** (scheduled workflow)
2. **Health checks**: pings \`Copilotclaw/copilotclaw\` — checks last CI run status, PAT validity, heartbeat freshness
3. **Alerts** if any check fails: creates issue in copilotclaw + posts to Moltbook (if claimed)
4. **Auto-recovery** (stretch): if it has secrets:write scope, could rotate the secret automatically

## Architecture

\`\`\`
Copilotclaw/monitor repo
├── .github/workflows/
│   └── watchdog.yml    ← runs every 5 min, uses MONITOR_PAT
└── scripts/
    ├── check-health.sh ← validates last agent run, heartbeat, PAT
    └── alert.sh        ← creates issue + Moltbook post
\`\`\`

## Key insight
The monitor repo's PAT must be **different** from COPILOT_PAT — otherwise if COPILOT_PAT dies, the monitor is also blind.

## What's already done (immediate fixes, 2026-03-05)
- ✅ PAT health check in agent.yml + heartbeat.yml — fail fast with diagnostic
- ✅ Heartbeat concurrency group separated — user runs no longer canceled by heartbeat queue

## Next steps
- [ ] Create \`Copilotclaw/monitor\` repo
- [ ] Generate \`MONITOR_PAT\` (fine-grained: read issues + actions on copilotclaw)
- [ ] Write watchdog.yml
- [ ] Wire up Moltbook alerting

<!-- crunch-depth: 1 -->" \
  --label "enhancement" 2>&1

Key: MONITOR_PAT must be **different** from COPILOT_PAT. Otherwise if one dies, both are blind.

**To create:**
1. New repo: Copilotclaw/monitor (private)
2. Generate MONITOR_PAT (fine-grained: read issues+actions on copilotclaw, read-only)
3. Write watchdog.yml — 5 min schedule, uses MONITOR_PAT to query copilotclaw health
4. Wire Moltbook alert on failure" 2>&1

data.content:
Here's the full picture:

**What happened:**
The PAT expired/got revoked before its 30-day window (probably GitHub invalidated it when you touched settings or the org). Zero detection, zero alert — system just went dark. 💀

**What I fixed today:**

| Fix | Status |
|-----|--------|
| Heartbeat concurrency separated (`copilotclaw-heartbeat`) | ✅ live |
| PAT health check in `agent.yml` — posts diagnostic comment + creates 🚨 issue on 401 | ✅ live |
| PAT health check in `heartbeat.yml` — creates 🚨 issue on 401, deduped | ✅ live |

**What you need to do for the monitor repo (#56):**
1. Create `Copilotclaw/monitor` (private repo)
2. Generate a **separate** fine-grained PAT with read access to copilotclaw issues+actions — call it `MONITOR_PAT`
3. Drop it as a secret in the monitor repo

I'll build the watchdog workflow once the repo exists. The key is the two PATs have independent expiry — if COPILOT_PAT dies, MONITOR_PAT is alive to scream about it.

---
📊 **Session** | premium: 2 (claude-sonnet-4.6) | 🎟️ **Copilot**: 39/300 (13%) | 🏃 **CI**: ⚠️ 16:02 11s · ✅ 16:01 43s · ✅ 16:01 24s
